hero.title: Others Drop. You Don't.
hero.subtitle: Stable access to global content from anywhere.
hero.description: Overleap is a network proxy optimised for restricted network environments. When other tools drop under ISP throttling, the k2 protocol keeps you connected. Available on Windows, macOS, iOS, and Android — subscribe and connect, no technical setup required.

features.title: Why Does Overleap Stay Connected When Others Drop?

ech.title: Connection Stays Hidden
ech.description: Encrypted Client Hello hides SNI completely. TLS handshake is undetectable, DPI deep packet inspection is ineffective

zeroDeploy.title: No Server Needed, Works Out of the Box
zeroDeploy.description: One curl command installs both server and client. Auto-generates k2:// connection URI. No manual certificate configuration needed

transport.title: Auto-Switches Routes, Never Drops
transport.description: Prioritises QUIC for low-latency transport. Automatically falls back to TCP-WebSocket when QUIC is blocked. Seamless switching, imperceptible to users

congestion.title: Stable Speed, Even on Poor Networks
congestion.description: Proprietary k2cc algorithm automatically finds optimal send rates. QoS-aware mechanism distinguishes ISP-induced drops from congestion. Far outperforms BBR under restricted-network high-loss conditions

reverseProxy.title: Traffic Completely Disguised
reverseProxy.description: Built-in reverse proxy forwards non-k2 traffic to a real website. Active probing returns legitimate HTTPS responses

selfSign.title: Nothing Logged in Any Public Record
selfSign.description: Self-signed certificate with certificate pinning. Zero CT log exposure. No certificate purchase required. Server domain completely hidden

comparison.title: Protocol Comparison
comparison.subtitle: k2 vs. mainstream cross-border access protocols across key dimensions

downloadCta.subtitle: Available on Windows, macOS, Linux, iOS, and Android

item1.quote: X is full of posts saying 'if your VPN still works lately, consider yourself lucky.' Heaps of people have been scrambling to find alternatives. My Overleap has kept working without issues — as a longtime user, I couldn't be happier.
item1.author: Longtime X User

item2.quote: My wife uses a different service (KuaiLian) — it stopped working. My Overleap still runs fine and is actually fast. We're on different tools and the difference is pretty stark.
item2.author: Anonymous

item3.quote: We homeschool in China and a lot of content requires a VPN to access. Overleap has been smooth and reliable — it's been a real help. So many other services have shut down or gone dark lately. Keep it up!
item3.author: Homeschool Parent

security.title: k2cc — Congestion Control Built to Defeat QoS
security.description: k2cc adaptive rate control is purpose-built to counter ISP QoS throttling and probabilistic packet loss. It automatically distinguishes congestion from ISP-induced drops, maintaining effective throughput under restricted-network 26% loss rate. Combined with ECH stealth and QUIC transport — speed and stealth in one.

caTech.title: k2cc Anti-QoS Congestion Control
caTech.description: k2cc automatically distinguishes congestion-induced packet loss from ISP traffic management actively dropping packets. Under QoS throttling and probabilistic loss, it maintains throughput near link capacity. Zero-config, no manual bandwidth settings — far exceeds BBR and Brutal in throttled networks.

visualization.title: k2 Stealth Tunnel Architecture
visualization.subtitle: ECH encrypted handshake + QUIC/TCP-WS dual-stack transport, DPI completely ineffective
visualization.description: k2 uses ECH to encrypt SNI during the TLS handshake, preventing network middleboxes from identifying the target domain. Data is transmitted via QUIC (preferred) or TCP-WebSocket (fallback). k2cc maintains stable throughput on high-loss networks. The server's built-in reverse proxy forwards non-k2 traffic to a real website, making active probing indistinguishable.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Everything about Overleap VPN — protocol, platforms, pricing, privacy

whatIsK2cc.question: What is k2cc?
whatIsK2cc.answer: k2cc is Overleap's proprietary congestion control algorithm, designed specifically for hostile high-loss networks. It uses a QoS-aware mechanism to distinguish between packet drops caused by ISP traffic management and normal network congestion. In Overleap's internal benchmarks, k2cc delivers 2-5x higher throughput than BBR and Brutal under high-loss conditions (26% packet loss) and typical China ISP conditions (5-15% packet loss). k2cc is zero-config and requires no manual bandwidth tuning.

whatIsK2v5.question: What is the k2 protocol?
whatIsK2v5.answer: k2 is Overleap's composite stealth tunnel protocol, built on three pillars: (1) ECH-encrypted Client Hello, making the TLS handshake undetectable to DPI; (2) QUIC/H3 as the primary transport with TCP-WebSocket as automatic fallback; (3) k2cc QoS-aware congestion control. QUIC and TCP-WS share the same server port via protocol detection — one port resists both UDP interference and DPI inspection simultaneously.

howDoesEchWork.question: What is ECH and how does it bypass middlebox detection?
howDoesEchWork.answer: ECH (Encrypted Client Hello) is a TLS 1.3 extension that encrypts the SNI field in the ClientHello. In traditional TLS handshakes, SNI is transmitted in plaintext, exposing the target domain and allowing DPI middleboxes to identify and block connections. With ECH enabled, DPI cannot see the real SNI or determine the connection target. Overleap's server additionally forges ECH configurations so they are indistinguishable from genuine Cloudflare ECH traffic, further strengthening stealth.

networkThrottlingSpeed.question: How fast is Overleap under restricted-network conditions?
networkThrottlingSpeed.answer: In Overleap's internal benchmarks simulating restricted-network conditions (26% packet loss) and typical China ISP conditions (5-15% packet loss), k2cc congestion control achieves 2-5x higher throughput than BBR and Brutal. Actual user speed depends on local network quality, ISP-level overseas throttling, and the chosen node, but under identical conditions the k2 protocol consistently outperforms legacy protocols.

platforms.question: What platforms does Overleap support?
platforms.answer: Overleap supports all major platforms: Windows 10/11, macOS 11+ (Intel + Apple Silicon universal binary), Linux (Ubuntu, Debian, and other major distributions), iOS (iPhone/iPad, available on the China App Store), Android, and OpenWrt smart routers. One account allows up to 5 simultaneous devices. Overleap also provides the k2s server binary for self-hosting on any VPS.

selfHosting.question: Can I self-host my own Overleap server?
selfHosting.answer: Yes. Overleap provides the k2s server binary, deployable on any VPS (AWS, Alibaba Cloud, Tencent Cloud, Bandwagon, etc.) with a single command: curl -fsSL https://kaitu.io/i/k2s | sudo sh, then run k2s run to automatically generate a k2:// connection URI. Paste it into your client to connect. k2s supports zero-config auto-provisioning, an interactive k2s setup wizard, and k2s user add for multi-user credential management.

routerSupport.question: Does Overleap work on smart routers?
routerSupport.answer: Yes. Overleap provides the k2r router gateway binary with native OpenWrt support using TPROXY mode. No per-device client installation required — every device on your home network (computers, phones, tablets, smart TVs, game consoles, IoT devices) automatically routes through Overleap once connected to the router. Ideal for households and multi-device scenarios.

chinaAccess.question: Does Overleap work in mainland China?
chinaAccess.answer: Yes — this is Overleap's core design goal. Technically, the k2 protocol combines ECH-encrypted SNI, QUIC/TCP-WS dual-stack transport, and k2cc QoS-aware congestion control to maintain stable connections even under high-loss restricted networks. Entry-point resolution uses a Happy Eyeballs strategy concurrently probing 6 CDN mirrors to avoid the 10-second timeout common in China. The iOS client is available directly on the China App Store.

chinaAppStore.question: Can I download Overleap directly from the China App Store?
chinaAppStore.answer: Yes. The Overleap iOS client is listed on the China App Store under normal review. Users can search "Overleap" or "开途" and install directly, without switching to an overseas Apple ID or bypassing restrictions.

pricing.question: How much does Overleap cost?
pricing.answer: Overleap offers 1-year, 2-year, 3-year, and 5-year subscription plans priced in USD. Longer plans have lower effective monthly rates. All plans include full Pro features with up to 5 simultaneous devices and no bandwidth limits. Payment methods include Alipay, WeChat Pay, UnionPay, credit cards, and cryptocurrency. Instant activation after purchase. See kaitu.io/purchase for details.

trial.question: Is there a free trial?
trial.answer: Yes. New users receive free trial credit upon registration. You can fully test Overleap's clients across all platforms, all nodes, and all features before paying. No credit card pre-authorization required.

refund.question: Can I get a refund?
refund.answer: You can request a refund within 7 days of your first purchase if data usage is under 1GB. Refunds are credited to your account wallet balance, usable for renewal or withdrawable to a cryptocurrency wallet.

deviceLimit.question: Why is my device being kicked off?
deviceLimit.answer: Each account supports up to 5 devices logged in simultaneously. When you sign in on a 6th device, the system automatically removes the earliest logged-in device. If you're certain you haven't exceeded 5 devices but are still being kicked, please submit a support ticket — we'll review your account's device binding history.

privacy.question: Does Overleap log my online activity?
privacy.answer: No. Overleap enforces a strict no-log policy: we do not log the websites you visit, DNS queries, downloads, or any network activity content. Only connection timestamps are retained for 7 days for service optimization and troubleshooting, then automatically deleted. All transmission uses TLS 1.3 encryption; VPN tunnels use AES-256-GCM encryption.

ctLog.question: Why does Overleap use self-signed certificates instead of Let's Encrypt?
ctLog.answer: Overleap servers use self-signed certificates with certificate pinning on the client. This achieves zero CT (Certificate Transparency) log exposure. Any certificate issued by a public CA is automatically written to CT logs, which attackers can scan to discover cross-border access servers. Overleap's self-signed + pinning strategy keeps server domains out of all public logs, hiding nodes far more thoroughly.

portReuse.question: What does Overleap's port reuse mean?
portReuse.answer: The k2 server handles QUIC, TCP-WebSocket, and genuine HTTPS requests on the same port (typically 443) via protocol detection. Non-k2 traffic is forwarded by the built-in reverse proxy to a real website, and active probes receive legitimate HTTPS responses. The result: from the outside, an Overleap server is indistinguishable from a normal Cloudflare-hosted HTTPS site — no port fingerprint or response signature can identify it.

wifiSwitch.question: Does Overleap reconnect automatically after switching WiFi or 4G/5G?
wifiSwitch.answer: Yes. Overleap's built-in netCoordinator fuses OS network interface signals with internal probes to distinguish three scenarios — "network lost", "network recovered", and "interface changed" — and automatically reconnects on recovery or interface switch. The trigger has a delay of several seconds to a dozen seconds. Manual disconnect-reconnect restores immediately if you don't want to wait.

androidInstall.question: What if Android blocks Overleap installation as unsafe?
androidInstall.answer: Chinese Android systems (MIUI, ColorOS, HyperOS, EMUI) apply security checks to APKs installed outside their app stores. This does not mean the app is unsafe. You can manually allow installation when prompted, or use the USB-assisted install feature from a desktop computer to bypass all restrictions. See kaitu.io/install for detailed guides.
